__2: Duplication of Transactions
__31:
1. Duplication of Outgoing Messages Duplicate outward messages attachment to WI were observed  Analysis: When the user initially clicked the Book Transaction button and received the response, they did not properly close the WorkItem. As a result, the WorkItem did not route to the Exit WorkStep as expected. Later, another user opened the same WorkItem and processed the transaction again, which caused the generated message to be attached twice.Resolution: Advise users to close WorkItems properly. This concern was raised and addressed previously as well.
 2. Duplication of Incoming Transactions Single incoming transactions generated two transaction IDs, resulting in double crediting in Finacle. 
 Analysis: When the STP_BookTransaction Utility tried to post the transaction got the Null response from the Finacle/OMNI, so the WorkItem get routed to the Checker WorkStep. But the the posting call done by STP_BookTransaction Utility processed the tranaction from the Finacle Backend. This causes the posting twice.Resolution : This issue is with finacle/OMNI side. 
3. Transaction Posting Failures (Unlien Error) Some transactions failed to post due to the below error: Sorry, we could not unlien your account despite liens already having been lifted in Finacle.Analysis: During the ERP integration deployment (UG, RW, TZ), an unapproved line of code was introduced, which was not part of the release build. This caused the unLien to fail.Resolution: After removing that line of code redeploying

__2: Issue in opening the Transaction Report under Finance Process
__29: Checked the Report Designer Query for that report and checked by running in the database first
__31: The query was reviewed and corrected by updating the data variable to match the appropriate DATETIME format required by the database. After making the necessary modifications, the query was revalidated to ensure accurate data retrieval. Post correction, the transaction report was successfully generated for the selected timeframe under the respective vendors and fields.

__2: Invalid field handling spotted at Credit and Debit grids under Finance Process
__29: Checked the logs for better understanding
__31: The database query used for fetching the Cost Center details was reviewed and corrected to ensure that the Cost Center codes are properly populated based on the selected Cost Center Description. The form-level configuration was validated to ensure the values are correctly reflected both within the grid and outside the grid fields. After implementing the necessary corrections and validating the functionality, the Cost Center Code and Description fields were successfully populated in the Credit and Debit grids.

__2: A Mukono branch user unable to find the authorizer under Assign To List
__29: Checked the Mukono branch users under master table
__31: The issue was analyzed and it was observed that the user Roderick Zesiro from Mukono Branch had incorrect username and email ID details configured in the User Master table. Due to this incorrect configuration, the user was unable to assign the transaction to the respective authorizer under the Operations process. The user details have been reviewed and corrected accordingly, and the user rights have been reverified to ensure proper role mapping and authorization flow. Post verification, the transaction assignment functionality is now expected to work as intended.

__2: Issue in Documents handling for Title or Corporate Search Process under Legal
__29: Checked the decision remarks, Document Upload and Field Handling logs for better understanding
__31: The issue was analyzed and it was identified that the document grid was not appearing during Initiation when the Type of Search was Corporate Search and the subcategory selected was Business Names under the Legal Process. The root cause was traced to the mandatory document handling logic implemented in the Java code, where the condition for this specific combination was not properly handled, resulting in the document grid not being displayed. The logic has now been reviewed and corrected to ensure that the document grid is properly rendered for this scenario. Post implementation of the fix, the documents are now visible as expected during the initiation stage.

__2: Namuwongo branch user unable to authorize the pending Transactions
__29: Checked the user master table and user configuration in Omni Docs
__31: The issue occurred due to an incorrect username entry along with the user account being disabled in OmniDocs, which prevented the user from authorizing pending transactions in the respective queue. The user configuration has been corrected and the account status has been addressed accordingly. Post correction, the user should now be able to access the queue and authorize the pending transactions without any issue.

__2: Issue in DE Transaction while the mode of payment is TT for Finance Process
__29: Checked the connectionURL logs and Workstep logs for better understanding
__31: The issue was identified to be caused by the Amount in UGX field accepting decimal values beyond the allowed precision, which resulted in transaction failure during Data Entry for International and Telephonic Transfers. To resolve this, a modification has been implemented in the function loadCreditGrid to restrict the Amount field to accept only 2 decimal place values. With this validation in place, the transaction should now process successfully without failure.

__2: Field handling issue for Credit grid under Finance Process
__29: Checked the fields from form and also checked the field validation logs
__31: The issue was caused due to incorrect handling in the JavaScript OnChange function triggered on changing the GL Description, which affected the auto-population of Amount and Currency in the Credit Grid during Journal Entry submission. The function has been re-verified and corrected to ensure proper handling for both fields. Post implementation, the Credit Grid now correctly auto-populates the Amount and Currency values as expected.

__2: Missing Document under Initiation for Collateral Valuation Process under Credit
__29: Checked the Decision Remarks, Document upload logs and Field Validation logs for better understanding
__31: The issue occurred because the document System Screenshot was not included in the Mandatory Documents configuration within the Java method handling document visibility at the Initiation stage for Collateral Valuation. The required document string has now been verified and included in the Mandatory Documents method in Java. After implementing this change, the document is now correctly visible under Mandatory Documents at Initiation.

After updating the configuration, the user should now be able to access the queue and proceed with testing the RTGS method under the Operations process in UAT.
